title: Market Brief — Friday, April 3, 2026
summary: GPU supply is split: H100s are in severe shortage at 15% availability (43/278), while H200s at 30% (127/417) and B200s at 35% (138/393) show loosening conditions as next-gen silicon absorbs demand. The biggest move is H200 spot dropping 4.5% overnight to $2.45/hr, signaling migration pressure off H100s into newer chips.
paragraphs: GPU supply is split: H100s are in severe shortage at 15% availability (43/278), while H200s at 30% (127/417) and B200s at 35% (138/393) show loosening conditions as next-gen silicon absorbs demand. The biggest move is H200 spot dropping 4.5% overnight to $2.45/hr, signaling migration pressure off H100s into newer chips.

At TCO-adjusted costs (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B costs $6.83/M on H100, $5.88/M on H200, and $4.58/M on B200. Medium-tier providers charging $3/M are selling at a loss of $2.88/M tokens on H200, making DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output and Meta Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60/M mathematically impossible without massive subsidy. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash.

OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued at 17x the cost of GPT-5 for incremental reasoning gains most workloads don't need. DeepSeek R1 at $2.50/M output on TCO-adjusted B200 cost of $4.58/M is undervalued if DeepSeek survives the cash burn, making it the asymmetric long against premium-tier incumbents.
bullets: 15% H100 availability means only 43 of 278 GPUs are free, qualifying as severe shortage.
35% B200 availability across 138 of 393 units marks the loosest compute class on the board.
$2.45/hr H200 spot median fell 4.5% day-over-day, the sharpest single-day GPU move tracked.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 implies a per-token loss of roughly $4.20/M against TCO-adjusted H200 cost.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token prices have fallen 79% since GPT-4 launch.
$80/M output for o3 Pro is 211x more expensive than DeepSeek V3.2 for comparable-class reasoning.
